mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,768k as of Aug 2014 shows ongoing employment growth
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year, the real interest rate is deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation and producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.22% and CPIAUCSL at 212.174 as of Sep 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with price stability
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered instances do not exceed baseline returns by > 150 bps on average, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 450,000 units signals weakening construction demand and forward-looking economic softness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 466,000 units as of Jul 2014
failure_condition: Negative HOUST signals fail to produce negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive, indicating supply-side constraints and margin compression that reduce forward equity returns over 20 trading days despite continued expansion.
failure_condition: Deceleration periods produce negative 20-day returns in < 48% of instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.0% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates below 100,000 persons monthly, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% and PAYEMS at 133,768k as of Aug 2014
regime_hint: labor market inflection point from recovery to stagnation
failure_condition: Joint deterioration signals fail to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while real GDP (GDPC1) remains in positive territory, indicating robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 346,810M and GDPC1 at 11,525B as of Aug 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer momentum
failure_condition: Accelerating retail sales fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at or below 0.25% for more than 12 consecutive months, indicating extended monetary accommodation that sustains equity valuations and produces persistently positive forward returns over 5 trading day intervals.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.22% as of Aug 2014, consistent with post-crisis accommodation
regime_hint: zero lower bound regime with extended quantitative easing
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during ZLB periods do not exceed non-ZLB baseline by > 50 bps on average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline > 0.3% AND UNRATE rise > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.337 and UNRATE at 8.1% as of Jul-Aug 2014
failure_condition: Joint deterioration signals fail to produce negative 20-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
